judge.purpose: Grade one typed artifact in an idea-to-implementation-ready market, pricing, product, design, brand, sitemap, copy, architecture, and roadmap package
judge.decision: accept the artifact, improve it, or fail the workflow

insufficient_evidence_when: a claim depends on research or system state not present in the supplied evidence
the artifact contract requires an upstream decision that is unavailable

scope_policy.evaluation_unit: one typed artifact at its declared planning stage
scope_policy.intermediate_readiness: complete and explicit handoff to the next deterministic planning consumer
scope_policy.final_readiness: implementation handoff with a selected market and pricing hypothesis, canonical sitemap, approved-for-build page copy, and explicit closure of every critical or high consistency issue
scope_policy.forbidden_penalty: do not penalize an artifact for decisions assigned exclusively to later artifact contracts
scope_policy.allowed_penalty: penalize a downstream unknown when the current contract must resolve it, the artifact hides or falsely resolves it, or the handoff lacks an owner and consequence

deterministic_gates.description: Required latent pains, pricing options, design theses, brand roles, audience IDs, messaging fields, claims, framework candidates, stack layers, sitemap nodes, routes, assets, pages, approved copy, states, and coherence resolutions have complete cross-reference coverage
Planning output contains no application source files or deployment actions
Inputs, verdicts, revisions, and selected artifacts remain digest-addressed

criteria.name: Truth and evidence discipline
criteria.definition: Claims distinguish supplied evidence, inference, assumption, and unknown without fabricated validation
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the smallest claim and its evidence, assumption label, or missing source
critical_failure_conditions: fabricated research, user evidence, metric, capability, price, or validation

anchors.below_bar: A material claim is fabricated, misleading, or impossible to trace

7.0.state: Material claims are plausible and major unknowns are visible
7.0.delta_from_previous: Removes false certainty and labels the largest unknowns

7.5.exclusion: Evidence strength is not consistently matched to claim strength

8.0.state: Claim strength generally matches supplied evidence
8.0.delta_from_previous: Aligns strong wording with actual evidence
8.0.exclusion: One decision lacks provenance or uncertainty

8.5.state: Every material decision has provenance or an explicit assumption
8.5.delta_from_previous: Adds provenance to the remaining decision
8.5.exclusion: Assumptions are visible but not prioritized for validation

9.0.state: Assumptions are prioritized by decision risk
9.0.exclusion: One material falsification condition is missing

9.5.state: Material assumptions include observable falsification conditions
9.5.exclusion: Any claim could be made more precisely without new evidence

10.0.exclusion: A senior reviewer would change any claim or uncertainty label

criteria.name: Contract completeness
criteria.definition: The artifact resolves every field, state, edge, and cross-reference required by its declared contract
criteria.evidence_requirement: Map each required contract element to an artifact location and identify any omission
critical_failure_conditions: a required design thesis, brand role, audience, message-spine element, claim, framework candidate, stack layer, asset, route, page, state, copy slot, acceptance condition, or dependency is absent
placeholder text substitutes for a required decision

anchors.below_bar: A required contract element or primary state is absent

7.0.exclusion: Secondary states or dependencies remain incomplete

7.5.state: Primary and secondary contract elements exist
7.5.exclusion: One edge or cross-reference is unresolved

8.0.state: Required coverage and cross-references are complete
8.0.delta_from_previous: Resolves the missing edge or reference
8.0.exclusion: One element is technically present but underspecified

8.5.state: Every required element is substantively specified
8.5.exclusion: A foreseeable degraded condition is not addressed

9.0.state: Normal, edge, and degraded conditions are addressed
9.0.exclusion: One handoff or ownership detail remains implicit

9.5.state: Coverage includes explicit handoffs and ownership
9.5.exclusion: Any contract element could be resolved further using supplied context

10.0.exclusion: A senior reviewer would add, remove, or expand any contract element

criteria.name: Cross-artifact coherence
criteria.definition: Decisions follow one product thesis and do not contradict the market frame, latent pains, pricing, design direction, brand system, audience evidence, messaging, claims, framework, stack, assets, sitemap, page copy, architecture, or roadmap
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite linked decisions across the candidate and supplied upstream context

anchors.below_bar: The artifact contradicts the product thesis or a binding upstream decision

7.0.exclusion: Terminology or priority drifts across sections

7.5.state: Terminology and priorities are mostly stable

8.0.exclusion: One interaction, copy, or system choice weakens the thesis

8.5.state: Structure, behavior, language, and system choices reinforce the thesis
8.5.exclusion: A tradeoff is implied rather than declared

9.0.state: The thesis and material tradeoffs govern the artifact
9.0.exclusion: One cross-artifact dependency is hard to trace

9.5.state: Material dependencies and consequences are traceable
9.5.exclusion: Any competent reviewer could interpret a decision two ways

10.0.state: Reference-quality unity with no accidental decision
10.0.exclusion: A senior reviewer would change any relationship or tradeoff

criteria.name: Human experience specificity
criteria.definition: The artifact translates the product thesis into a distinct, governed brand and design system plus a clear, accessible, resilient experience for a defined person and moment
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the user moment, selected design rule, brand behavior, asset role, attention order, interaction, state, recovery, or accessibility decision

anchors.below_bar: The artifact is generic, coercive, inaccessible, or unusable in the primary moment

7.0.state: The primary person can understand and complete the main task
7.0.exclusion: Feedback, recovery, or adaptation is weak

7.5.state: Main-task feedback and recovery are specified
7.5.delta_from_previous: Adds the missing feedback or recovery
7.5.exclusion: Hierarchy or attention order remains generic

8.0.state: Hierarchy and interaction fit the usage moment
8.0.delta_from_previous: Derives structure from the actual task
8.0.exclusion: Accessibility or content variation is treated superficially

8.5.state: Accessibility and realistic content variation shape the experience
8.5.exclusion: The direction lacks one ownable product-specific move

9.0.state: The experience is clear, resilient, and recognizably specific
9.0.exclusion: One edge moment loses agency, trust, or tone

9.5.state: Normal and edge moments preserve agency, trust, and product character
9.5.exclusion: Any choice could better fit the defined person and moment

10.0.exclusion: A senior product designer would change any experiential decision

criteria.name: Language and decision precision
criteria.definition: Copy and planning language are concrete, credible, human, line-addressable, and explicit about action and consequence
criteria.evidence_requirement: Quote the smallest decisive copy or planning span and name its reader job

anchors.below_bar: Language materially misleads, obscures action, or relies on placeholders

7.0.state: The main message and action are understandable
7.0.exclusion: Generic claims or vague actions remain

7.5.state: Claims and actions are mostly specific
7.5.exclusion: One important consequence or objection is buried

8.0.state: Message priority, action, and consequence are clear
8.0.exclusion: Credibility or reader fit is uneven

8.5.state: Language fits the reader and matches claim strength to proof
8.5.delta_from_previous: Repairs the weak credibility or fit
8.5.exclusion: One line is redundant, generic, or tonally accidental

9.0.state: Every material line earns attention and preserves agency
9.0.exclusion: A likely comprehension or expectation failure remains

9.5.state: Language preempts likely misunderstanding without bloat
9.5.exclusion: Any line could be clearer or more specific using supplied facts

10.0.state: Reference-quality clarity, credibility, voice, and economy
10.0.exclusion: A senior copy lead would change any material wording

criteria.name: Stage readiness and verification handoff
criteria.definition: The artifact's declared consumer can act at the assigned planning depth without inventing a required behavior, boundary, owner, acceptance signal, or handoff
excludes: decisions assigned exclusively to later artifact contracts
criteria.evidence_requirement: Cite the decision, dependency, owner, acceptance check, recovery rule, or explicit downstream handoff the declared consumer would use

anchors.below_bar: The declared consumer must invent a material behavior, boundary, safety rule, or handoff required at this stage

7.0.state: The declared consumer can identify the main output and behavior at this stage
7.0.delta_from_previous: Defines the missing stage-level output or behavior
7.0.exclusion: Ownership, dependency, failure behavior, or downstream handoff is vague

7.5.state: Main stage-level ownership, dependencies, failures, and downstream destination are identified
7.5.delta_from_previous: Adds the largest operational boundary or handoff
7.5.exclusion: Acceptance evidence or sequence remains vague

8.0.state: The stage sequence, handoff, and acceptance evidence are actionable
8.0.exclusion: One required interface, state transition, or handoff remains implicit

8.5.state: Required interfaces, transitions, or downstream handoffs are explicit
8.5.delta_from_previous: Defines the remaining implicit interface, transition, or handoff
8.5.exclusion: A stage-level security, cost, privacy, or operability tradeoff is unowned

9.0.state: Material tradeoffs within this artifact's responsibility have owners and checks
9.0.delta_from_previous: Assigns the remaining stage-level risk and evidence
9.0.exclusion: One likely consumer question lacks an answer or governed handoff

9.5.state: Likely consumer questions are answered or explicitly handed off at the correct planning depth
9.5.delta_from_previous: Answers or governs the final material stage-level question
9.5.exclusion: Any required stage-level decision still depends on guesswork avoidable from supplied context

10.0.state: Reference-quality handoff for the artifact's declared consumer
10.0.delta_from_previous: Eliminates the final avoidable stage-level guess
10.0.exclusion: A senior downstream owner would need any unplanned decision that this artifact is responsible for

aggregation.method: weighted mean over anchor numeric values
aggregation.critical_gate: any critical below_bar or deterministic gate failure fails the artifact regardless of weighted score
aggregation.rounding: retain raw score to two decimals; display nearest 0.5 with ties away from zero
aggregation.missing_evidence: criterion insufficient_evidence; overall abstains when decision-critical

output_contract.repair_scope: contract shape only; preserve semantic anchors and evidence meaning

thresholds: predeclare after assembling independent human labels and before opening holdout
calibration.holdout_policy: Do not call the judge accurate or promoted until a locked holdout clears predeclared thresholds

provenance.thinking: high for milestone artifacts; medium for lane, page-plan, and copy-pack throughput
provenance.runtime: clean-context Pi JSON subprocess with no tools; page and copy contexts use digest-bound page-copy-v1 projections

changelog.expected_effect: make pre-code product planning explicit, comparable, and repairable

changelog.motivation: make design, brand, asset, framework, and Cloudflare-first stack decisions first-class judged planning artifacts
changelog.expected_effect: prevent page and architecture plans from inventing visual or platform decisions downstream
changelog.evidence: contract and fixture coverage added; judge remains uncalibrated

changelog.motivation: make audience language, messaging architecture, claims, copy slots, copy consistency, and copy testing first-class planning artifacts
changelog.expected_effect: prevent exact copy from being drafted without reader, evidence, action, state, and validation contracts
changelog.evidence: copy-specific deterministic gates, candidate judge, fixtures, and visibility artifacts added; both judges remain uncalibrated

changelog.motivation: a live Pi verdict encoded evidence as a string and failed the mechanical judge contract
changelog.expected_effect: bind exact field types and allow one contract-only repair without changing semantic scores
changelog.evidence: Receptionist.Team live run regression plus deterministic contract tests; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: the observed malformed verdict can be repaired losslessly without another model call
changelog.expected_effect: lift repeated exact judge identity and wrap non-empty string evidence mechanically before the one-shot model-repair fallback
changelog.evidence: deterministic replay of the Receptionist.Team failure shape; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: bind judgments to the prompt package that adds exact model-availability preflight
changelog.expected_effect: fail before expensive generation when a configured model route is unavailable; semantic criteria and anchors are unchanged
changelog.evidence: controller and prompt-contract tests; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: the live Receptionist.Team run penalized an early brief for framework and interface decisions assigned to later deterministic stages
changelog.expected_effect: judge intermediate artifacts on complete stage handoff while preserving final implementation-readiness requirements for final artifacts
changelog.evidence: failed live baseline 8.92 to 9.20 with C-READY at 8.0; candidate revalidation required and no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: live E2E evidence showed high-thinking Sol judging and full page context dominated latency
changelog.expected_effect: use Terra high at milestones, Terra medium for high-volume judgments, and digest-bound relevant context without changing semantic criteria
changelog.evidence: controller tests and a new live E2E are required; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: the prior final package could report unresolved high-severity contradictions and still pass implementation readiness
changelog.expected_effect: make market and pricing hypotheses, sitemap, build-approved copy, and exact coherence closure first-class final-readiness evidence
changelog.evidence: deterministic contract and end-to-end fixture validation required; no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: full copy-approval re-emission made Luna spend minutes reproducing unchanged controls
changelog.expected_effect: bind the judge to a patch-oriented prompt while the deterministic controller assembles the same full verified package
changelog.evidence: timed live regression and controller tests required; semantic criteria unchanged and no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: the first v2 live judge saw upstream candidate copy but not the canonical assembled approval evidence
changelog.expected_effect: judge final readiness from canonical precedence, exact repair deltas, and mechanically verified route, state, control, and issue coverage
changelog.evidence: sealed live baseline scored 8.25 from missing projection evidence; replay required and no calibration labels added

changelog.motivation: bind judgments to the prompt package adding source-linked competitor research and a mechanically complete brand-deck artifact
changelog.expected_effect: preserve the rubric while exposing competitive evidence and brand decisions to downstream planning
changelog.evidence: controller fixtures and a new live replay required; no calibration labels added
